perturbation.original_text: Licensor and Licensee are parties to that certain Distributor Agreement dated as of January 1, 1996 (the "Distributor Agreement"). Licensor and Licensee desire to amend the Distributor Agreement as set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensor and Licensee are parties to that certain Distributor Agreement dated as of January 1, 1996 (the "Distributor Agreement"). Licensor and NEON desire to amend the Distributor Agreement as set forth herein.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Licensee' is replaced with 'NEON'. While NEON Systems, Inc. is defined as Licensee, using the specific company name inconsistently creates confusion. It raises a question: is there a distinction being made in this specific context? This contradiction directly affects the understanding of who is amending the agreement and can lead to disputes over obligations and rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RECITALS: Licensor and Licensee are parties to that certain Distributor Agreement dated as of January 1, 1996 (the "Distributor Agreement"). Licensor and NEON desire to amend the Distributor Agreement as set forth herein. NOW, THEREFORE, for and consideration of the mutual covenants of the parties set forth herein, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the parties hereto hereby agree as follow:
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Licensee' with 'NEON' while the original agreement refers to 'Licensee'. This introduces ambiguity, as the contract now inconsistently refers to the same party using two different names, especially since the Contradicted Text also includes both terms, which can cause confusion on whether 'Licensee' and 'NEON' are the same entity in all contexts within the agreement. This falls under the 'Misaligned Terminology' - In Text Contradiction definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: Section 3.1 ROYALTIES TO LICENSOR. (a) Licensee shall pay to Licensor for each Licensed Product licensed to a Redistributor or a Customer a royalty equal to the Specified Royalty Percentage of all revenues received (without deduction for value added tax, if any, but excluding any revenues for maintenance and support or upgrade services, which revenues are covered in paragraph (b) below) by Licensee under the Redistributor Agreement or Sublicense applicable to such Licensed Product.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 3.1 ROYALTIES TO LICENSOR. (a) NEON shall pay to Licensor for each Licensed Product licensed to a Redistributor or a Client a royalty equal to the Specified Royalty Percentage of all revenues received (without deduction for value added tax, if any, but excluding any revenues for maintenance and support or upgrade services, which revenues are covered in paragraph (b) below) by NEON under the Redistributor Agreement or Sublicense applicable to such Licensed Product.
perturbation.explanation: Here, the term 'Licensee' is interchanged with 'NEON', and 'Customer' is replaced with 'Client'. This creates uncertainty about whether 'NEON' always refers to the 'Licensee' in all instances, and if 'Client' has the same meaning as 'Customer', especially in the context of royalty payments. This in-text contradiction creates ambiguity regarding to whom royalties are owed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 3.1 ROYALTIES TO LICENSOR. (a) NEON shall pay to Licensor for each Licensed Product licensed to a Redistributor or a Client a royalty equal to the Specified Royalty Percentage of all revenues received (without deduction for value added tax, if any, but excluding any revenues for maintenance and support or upgrade services, which revenues are covered in paragraph (b) below) by NEON under the Redistributor Agreement or Sublicense applicable to such Licensed Product. (b) Licensee shall pay to Licensor for maintenance and support and upgrade services provided under the applicable Sublicense or other written maintenance and support agreement with or approved by Licensee for each of the Licensed Products a royalty equal to the Specified Royalty Percentage of all revenues received (without deduction for value added tax, if any) by Licensee from a Redistributor or Customer relating to maintenance and support services or services for Upgrades or upgrades of systems for such Licensed Products.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Licensee' to 'NEON' and 'Customer' to 'Client' in Section 3.1(a), while seemingly minor, introduces ambiguity that creates a contradiction. The contradicted text, Section 3.1(b), explicitly states 'Licensee shall pay...from a Redistributor or Customer'. If 'NEON' is not explicitly and always defined as 'Licensee' and 'Client' not explicitly and always defined as 'Customer', it creates confusion regarding who is responsible for payments from 'Customer' since the modified text now attributes revenue to 'Client' while subsection (b) still references Customer. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology, where the interchange of terms without clear definition leads to conflicting obligations and uncertainty about who owes royalties under which circumstance, thus there is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. AMENDMENT OF TERMINATION PROVISIONS. The provisions of Section 14.2 are amended by deleting in its entirety subsection (b) thereof and by amending Section 14.2(a)(2) to read in its entirety as follows: (2) Immediately upon written notice if the other party defaults in the performance of any obligation under this Agreement, including failure to promptly pay any amount due hereunder, and fails to cure such default within thirty (30) days after delivery of written notice specifying the default (with any termination as a result of Licensee's failure to pay amounts due under this Agreement resulting in acceleration of Licensee's obligation to pay all sums accrued and payable to Licensor under this Agreement as of the date of such termination).
perturbation.changed_text: 4. AMENDMENT OF TERMINATION PROVISIONS. The provisions of Section 14.2 are amended by deleting in its entirety subsection (b) thereof and by amending Section 14.2(a)(2) to read in its entirety as follows: (2) Immediately upon written notice if the other party defaults in the performance of any obligation under this Agreement, including failure to promptly pay any amount due hereunder, and fails to cure such default within thirty (30) days after delivery of written notice specifying the default (with any termination as a result of NEON's failure to pay amounts due under this Agreement resulting in acceleration of Licensee's obligation to pay all sums accrued and payable to Licensor under this Agreement as of the date of such termination).
perturbation.explanation: The text replaced 'Licensee' for 'NEON', creating an in-text contradiction in the termination conditions. It now reads '...termination as a result of NEON's failure to pay... resulting in acceleration of Licensee's obligation...'. This suggests that NEON and Licensee may not be the same entity, which will cause confusion when the time for interpreting the contract comes. This uncertainty can cause conflicts during enforcement about who is responsible for what obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. AMENDMENT OF TERMINATION PROVISIONS. The provisions of Section 14.2 are amended by deleting in its entirety subsection (b) thereof and by amending Section 14.2(a)(2) to read in its entirety as follows: (2) Immediately upon written notice if the other party defaults in the performance of any obligation under this Agreement, including failure to promptly pay any amount due hereunder, and fails to cure such default within thirty (30) days after delivery of written notice specifying the default (with any termination as a result of NEON's failure to pay amounts due under this Agreement resulting in acceleration of Licensee's obligation to pay all sums accrued and payable to Licensor under this Agreement as of the date of such termination).
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Licensee' with 'NEON' in Section 4. This creates a contradiction because the modified clause now states '...termination as a result of NEON's failure to pay... resulting in acceleration of Licensee's obligation...'. This implies that NEON and the Licensee are distinct entities, directly conflicting with the original agreement where the terms 'Licensee' and 'NEON' were intended to refer to the same party. This uncertainty regarding the obligated party constitutes a strong in-text contradiction, aligning with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
